steps.title: Look for recovery evidence
steps.content: The program's goal is to breed captive condors and reintroduce them into the wild. Data supporting reasonable success should show the wild population increasing overall and becoming strong relative to the captive population.

steps.title: Reject weak graph claims
steps.content:
• Choice A focuses on a captive decrease, which by itself does not show successful reintroduction.
  • Choice B describes a slight one-year decrease, not evidence of overall success.
  • Choice C focuses on a steady difference between groups, not on growth of wild condors.
The answer is D.
